description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a broad, curved steel blade and a wooden handle. The blade is wide and slightly convex, featuring a pronounced cutting edge and a subtle beard that extends downward. The handle is made of brown wood, gently curved for ergonomic grip, and appears to be solid and robust. The blade is mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged style, with the head fitting snugly at the top. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the blade or the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or suburban environment.
description: A broad, curved steel-bladed axe with a solid, gently curved wooden handle and a traditional wedged mounting. The design is simple and functional, with no visible markings.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the side, highlighting the full profile of the wide, curved blade and the brown wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, showing the thinness of the blade and the handle, with minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile of the axe, again displaying the broad blade and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the other edge, emphasizing the slim profile of the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle, oval cross-section, smooth surface, ergonomic grip, no additional grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad curved blade with a convex edge and a slight beard, no decorative features, functional design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the top of the wooden handle.
